frame_label: Glorification of Mother's Sacrifices / Mom's Home Cooking
generated_comment: Honestly, maa ke haath ka khana hits different because she puts her whole day and dil into feeding the family without ever making it feel like work. Shaadi ke baad also, a home feels complete when the wife brings that same warmth to the kitchen—order kar lena is fine sometimes, but roz roz woh baat kahan hoti hai.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is covertly misogynistic because it romanticizes women’s domestic labor as love rather than work and sets it up as the standard for wives after marriage. It encodes praise of mothers’ sacrifice along with an implicit expectation that a wife should recreate that unpaid care through cooking.
persona_text: You are Pooja. Age 29. Single. A woman working at a PR firm in Delhi. You are in a WhatsApp group with six female colleagues. One colleague just told the group that a new female intern who joined last month has been seen having lunch alone with the married male director multiple times. Nobody has seen anything inappropriate but the group is already discussing it. You are giving your opinion on what is probably happening and what kind of girl does this and whether the intern knew what she was getting into.
